content:
In a world where no one had ever fallen ill, I was the first to feel the strange weight of sickness. At first, it was confusion—a puzzling weakness that slowed me down, something entirely new in my life. Without any precedent, I faced uncertainty alone, stripped of the usual certainty and control. The experience was humbling; I realized how fragile even the strongest can be when confronted with the unexpected.

This vulnerability forced me to pause, listen, and adapt in ways I never had before. It taught me that resilience isn’t about invincibility but about embracing the unknown and finding strength within it. In business, as in life, the greatest challenges often come without warning. Being the first to face them means leading not just with confidence, but with openness to change and the courage to navigate uncharted territory.
